betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Knowledge is power. Truth is power.
Absoute power.
Literal power.
The power of the True Tongue, as it was once wielded by my predecessor.
Order, as many other virtuous claims, inevitably encounters opposition. 
An opposition that, looking at my own preconceptions, my own imperfections, my own misunderstandings, could be entirely legitimate.
Or an opposition that is driven by Chaos, and that must be fought.
Knowing when to fight and who to fight is not an easy challenge, nor one that is solved once and for all. It is a constant struggle, and one where I most likely will be alone.
I cannot claim that all of the battles I have done fought are perfectly righteous, or even that they were committed for righteous reasons. And yet I have to contend with them
This is also part of the power of truth. In the embracing of my mission, I must contend with the fact, the inevitability, that despite my best intentions, I will fail.
And, in knowing my True Name, I can accept this truth, I can embrace it, and I can wield it as a wepon.
A weapon that now must be pointed towards the Lord of Gluttony, the Master of Chaos.
